channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Cao says industry needs to ‘move forward’ in first speech as acting SecNav | Breaking Defense
item.description: Acting Secretary of the Navy Hung Cao delivered his first public remarks since stepping into the role.

item.title: 🌏 China Pulls the Plug on Meta’s AI Acquisition | Foreign Policy
item.description: The reversal underscores Beijing’s shifting national security concerns.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s Zelennials have youth on their side in war against Putin’s pensioners | Atlantic Council
item.description: It is no exaggeration to state that the current war in Ukraine is a clash of generations pitting Russia’s staunchly Soviet leadership against the radically different post-Soviet mindset of the Ukrainian authorities, writes Peter Dickinson. The post Ukraine’s Zelennials have…

item.title: 🏛️ Marine requirement for lightweight counter-drone tech to be released in coming months | Breaking Defense
item.description: Though there is not a current requirement or program of record for such systems, they have been fielded to Marines due to an “urgent field need.”

item.title: 📍 Timeline of Boat Strikes and Related Actions | Just Security
item.description: A timeline that chronicles major events in the Trump administration’s campaign of lethal strikes against suspected drug traffickers in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific. The post Timeline of Boat Strikes and Related Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 Iran war could leave Ukrainian civilians exposed to Russian missiles | Atlantic Council
item.description: With the Iran war depleting global stocks of Patriot air defense interceptor missiles, fears are mounting in Kyiv that Ukrainian civilians could be left exposed to Russian ballistic missiles during an anticipated summer bombing campaign, write Maksym Beznosiuk and William Dixon.…

item.title: 🌏 ASPI’s China Defence Universities Tracker: China–Iran research ties | ASPI Strategist
item.description: China and Iran have gradually deepened research ties in dual-use and emerging technologies over the past 15 years, the latest update to ASPI’s China Defence Universities Tracker (CDUT) shows. The scale of this collaboration is ...

item.title: 🏛️ Navy’s drone refueler notches first flight—but it won’t be on aircraft carriers until 2029 | Defense One
item.description: The MQ-25A delay pushes deliveries another two years past the prior reschedule.

item.title: 🏛️ Marines will update land warfare doctrine as they prep for near-peer, drone-driven fight | Defense One
item.description: Officials previewed Ground Combat Element 2040 plan at the Modern Day Marine conference.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon formally requests name change to War Department, setting up fight with Dems | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Defense Department stated in a legislative proposal that it will likely spend about $50 million in fiscal 2026 to get the ball rolling on a name change.

item.title: 🔐 Light Uncrewed Cargo Helicopters Based On Robinson R66 And Bell 505 Compete For USMC Contract | The War Zone
item.description: Adaptations of two proven commercial rotary-wing designs are vying for a U.S. Marine Corps autonomous resupply vehicle requirement. The post Light Uncrewed Cargo Helicopters Based On Robinson R66 And Bell 505 Compete For USMC Contract appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon’s FY27 budget seeks 85 F-35s, but most ride on reconciliation | Defense News
item.description: The Pentagon’s fiscal 2027 budget request seeks 85 F-35 Lightning II fighters across the joint force, up from 47 in fiscal 2026.

item.title: 🌍 Why Iran Isn’t Blinking Yet | Foreign Policy
item.description: Trump’s blockade aims to force damaging shutdowns at Iranian oil fields. But Tehran has been through this before.

item.title: 🌏 Japan and China Are Edging Dangerously Close to Conflict | Foreign Policy
item.description: Beijing is ready to take risks as Tokyo backs Taiwan.

item.title: 📍 Unmanned vessels in the water at Sea Air Space 2026 | Breaking Defense
item.description: This week on The Break Out, a tour of the Sea Air Space 2026 conference from scenic National Harbor, Md.

item.title: ⚛️ Navy Still Pushing To Field New AARGM-ER Radar-Busting Missile This Year Despite “Strategic Pause” | The War Zone
item.description: The Navy expects to see the AGM-88G AARGM-ER enter service in September despite not planning to buy any of them in the next fiscal year. The post Navy Still Pushing To Field New AARGM-ER Radar-Busting Missile This Year Despite “Strategic Pause” appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Australia boosts army with HIMARS and more armored vehicles | Breaking Defense
item.description: The HIMARS buy will come to $2.3 billion AUD, as the Australian military aims to “significantly enhance” its long-range strike capability.

item.title: ⚛️ India races to boost conventional, nuclear submarine combat punch | Defense News
item.description: The induction of a third SSBN brings the number of such boats to three, enabling the deployment of a continuous at-sea nuclear counterstrike force.

item.title: 🏛️ UK lawmaker report warns AUKUS under threat from ‘shortcomings and failings’ | Breaking Defense
item.description: Findings also claim that the Royal Navy’s Astute-class nuclear-powered submarines have been “stretched” to, or “even beyond,” their limits.

item.title: 🌏 Expanding Frontiers: China’s Military Push Beyond the First Island Chain | The Diplomat
item.description: Beijing has a deliberate, long-term strategy – one that prioritizes persistence and gradual advantage.

item.title: 🌏 Whether Kusong or Yongbyon, North Korea Will Never Give Up Its Nuclear Weapons Program | The Diplomat
item.description: A controversy over the South Korean unification minister’s comments exposes the illusion at the heart of decades of North Korea policy.

item.title: 📍 Accelerating space power through software-defined mission dominance | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] As space threats continue to evolve, existing satellites need new capabilities to defend and protect, even after launch.

item.title: 📍 The 2026 Global Energy Agenda | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Atlantic Council is pleased to present its sixth Global Energy Agenda that shares new insights from thought leaders and our annual survey of experts from around the world. The post The 2026 Global Energy Agenda appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: ⚛️ Nuclear energy for an era of rising demand and strategic risk | Atlantic Council
item.description: As electricity demand and geopolitical tensions rise, nuclear energy stands out as a crucial pillar in countries' energy security strategies. The post Nuclear energy for an era of rising demand and strategic risk appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 The infrastructure that will drive energy innovation and competitiveness | Atlantic Council
item.description: Companies across industries are investing in lower-carbon production. What's needed now to drive competition and innovation is the right carbon measurement system. The post The infrastructure that will drive energy innovation and competitiveness appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🏛️ Is the Indian Army Ready for the Next War? | The Diplomat
item.description: Col. Ajai Shukla discusses India’s Army modernization and reform post-Sindoor.

item.title: 📍 High Oil Prices Are Devastating Island States – and Moving the Needle on Climate | The Diplomat
item.description: For many small island states, the abstract concept of a “polycrisis” has become a dangerously tangible threat.

item.title: 🏛️ Army releases commercial solutions opening for rapid EW and signals intelligence capabilities | Breaking Defense
item.description: The commercial solutions offering aims to produce a “library” that will provide options for units to determine what capabilities meet their structure and mission.

item.title: 🌏 The Perils of South Korea’s Digital Diplomacy | The Diplomat
item.description: Lee Jae-myung’s post on X marked a new beginning for South Korea’s digital diplomacy. It immediately caused a diplomatic row.

item.title: 📍 Golden Dome has ‘pathways to pivot’ if delays arise, general says | Defense News
item.description: Gen. Michael Guetlein, the appointed czar for America's “Golden Dome” initiative, told lawmakers that plans to build the shield were proceeding apace.
